title: Emotional Dysregulation Self-Check
description: This brief self-check helps you reflect on patterns of emotional intensity, reactivity, and difficulty managing strong emotions. It is not a diagnostic tool — it's designed to increase self-awareness and help you identify which DBT skills might be most helpful for you.
instructions: For each statement below, choose the response that best describes your experience over the past 2 weeks. Answer honestly — there are no right or wrong answers.

questions.text: My emotions felt very intense — much stronger than the situation seemed to call for
My mood shifted quickly and unpredictably, sometimes multiple times in a day
Once I got upset, it took a long time to calm down and return to baseline
I felt emotionally overwhelmed — like my feelings were too much to handle
I acted impulsively when upset (said things I regretted, spent money, ate/drank too much, self-harmed)
I tried to avoid, suppress, or numb my emotions (through distraction, substances, isolation, or shutting down)
My emotional reactions caused problems in my relationships
I was highly self-critical or felt ashamed about my emotional reactions

bands.label: Generally manageable
bands.description: Your emotional patterns seem generally within a manageable range. The DBT skills in this course can still strengthen your emotional resilience and help you navigate challenging situations more effectively.

bands.label: Moderate difficulty
bands.description: You're experiencing some difficulty managing emotional intensity. The skills in this course — particularly mindfulness, distress tolerance, and emotion labeling — can make a meaningful difference. Pay attention to which situations trigger the strongest reactions.

bands.label: Significant difficulty
bands.description: Emotional dysregulation is notably affecting your life and relationships. We encourage you to work through this course AND consider professional support — a therapist trained in DBT can provide personalized skills coaching. The skills you'll learn here are the same ones used in clinical DBT.

bands.label: Serious difficulty
bands.description: Your answers suggest intense emotional dysregulation that is causing serious problems. We strongly encourage you to seek professional support. A DBT-trained therapist can provide the structured skills training, coaching, and validation that makes the biggest difference. If emotional dysregulation is leading to self-harm or suicidal thoughts, please contact a crisis resource immediately.

functionalImpairmentQuestion.text: How much has difficulty managing emotions interfered with your daily life, relationships, or work over the past 2 weeks?

disclaimer: This self-check is for educational self-reflection only. It is not a clinical diagnostic tool and does not constitute medical or psychological advice. If you are engaging in self-harm or having thoughts of suicide, please reach out immediately: call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline), text HOME to 741741 (Crisis Text Line), or call 911 for emergencies.
